question: does structured pruning drag a searched OFA subnet under the baseline latency?
verdict: Pruning reduces latency monotonically. fp32: r>=0.40 (64% params) beats baseline. fp16 (deployment): only r=0.60 (84% params) beats baseline; crossover ~r=0.48 (~73% params). Every rung meets 60 FPS even unpruned (81 FPS). To beat the fp16 baseline the graft must be gutted to 84% param sparsity from a 0.61 unpruned proxy -> accuracy-dominated by the dense arm (prune_base_* beats baseline fp16 at 0.83+ mAP). Latency measured-only (off LUT grid).
finding: pruning is a real but weak lever for the memory-bound graft: the pruned e2e beat the channel-linear prior (head/adapter prune too, 1x1s drop super-linearly), yet 84% of params must go to beat the fp16 baseline. The two supernet gates now closed by measurement bracket the axis: MBv3 graft memory-bound (this + Stage 0), R50 compute-bound (screen_r50); yolo11's dense-moderate blocks are the optimum.
next: optional CP 6.2 accuracy check: recovery-train r=0.60 (the only fp16 latency-winner) on a GPU session and measure pose mAP. Prior: loses to the dense family. If confirmed, the dense arm remains the deploy path; if it surprises, it is a NAS-produced net under the baseline.
